title: Ensure Legacy Authorization (ABAC) is Disabled

input_row.title: Ensure Legacy Authorization (ABAC) is Disabled
input_row.description: Legacy Authorization, also known as Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) has been superseded by Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and is not under active development. RBAC is the recommended way to manage permissions in Kubernetes.
input_row.rationale: In Kubernetes, RBAC is used to grant permissions to resources at the cluster and namespace level. RBAC allows the definition of roles with rules containing a set of permissions, whilst the legacy authorizer (ABAC) in Kubernetes Engine grants broad, statically defined permissions. As RBAC provides significant security advantages over ABAC, it is recommended option for access control. Where possible, legacy authorization must be disabled for GKE clusters.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. From the list of clusters, click on each cluster to open the Details pane, and make sure 'Legacy Authorization' is set to 'Disabled'. Using Command Line: To check Legacy Authorization status for an existing cluster, first define 2 variables for Cluster Name and Zone and then run the following command: gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE -- format json | jq '.legacyAbac' The output should return null null if Legacy Authorization is Disabled. If Legacy Authorization is Enabled, the above command will return true value. Internal Only - General
input_row.remediation: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. Select Kubernetes clusters for which Legacy Authorization is enabled. 3. Click EDIT. 4. Set 'Legacy Authorization' to 'Disabled'. 5. Click SAVE. Using Command Line: To disable Legacy Authorization for an existing cluster, run the following command: gcloud container clusters update --zone --no- enable-legacy-authorization
input_row.impact: Once the cluster has the legacy authorizer disabled, the user must be granted the ability to create authorization roles using RBAC to ensure that the role-based access control permissions take effect.
input_row.default_value: Kubernetes Engine clusters running GKE version 1.8 and later disable the legacy authorization system by default, and thus role-based access control permissions take effect with no special action required.
input_row.additional_information: On clusters running GKE 1.6 or 1.7, Kubernetes Service accounts have full permissions on the Kubernetes API by default. To ensure that the role-based access control permissions take effect for a Kubernetes service account, the cluster must be created or updated with the option --no-enable-legacy-authorization. This requirement is removed for clusters running GKE version 1.8 or higher.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control on disabling Legacy Authorization (ABAC) can be fully verified using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). The audit instructions already include a command that utilizes gcloud to obtain the status of Legacy Authorization.
- The presence of a CLI command indicates that this control is queryable through the appropriate APIs, meeting other decision criteria.
- The control is framed around access control, which is a standard feature of GCP resources.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_legacy_authorization_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the status of Legacy Authorization on GKE clusters using the following methods:
- Use the command: 
  ```
  gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --format json
  ```
  to retrieve the cluster configuration in JSON format, focusing on the `legacyAbac` field.

- The exact configuration property to validate will be:
  - `legacyAbac` should be `null` (which indicates disabled) or explicitly set to `false`.
  
- **Pass Criteria:** If `legacyAbac` returns `null` or `false`, the control is considered compliant.
- **Fail Criteria:** If `legacyAbac` returns `true`, the control is considered non-compliant and Legacy Authorization is enabled.

The program should enumerate all the clusters within a specified project or region to check their configurations.
